summary: Jeff Atwood introduces a client-side JavaScript syntax highlighting solution he discovered on Alex Gorbatchev's blog. After expressing frustration with the primitive PRE tags on his own blog and reluctance to hack Perl-based solutions into Movable Type, he found Alex's dp.SyntaxHighlighter to be an elegant alternative. The tool supports multiple languages including C#, VB.NET, JavaScript, PHP, SQL, and XML. Jeff shares a VB.NET code sample demonstrating the highlighter in action and notes that browser compatibility testing is ongoing.
key_points: Client-side JavaScript offers a cleaner alternative to server-side syntax highlighting for blogs
The dp.SyntaxHighlighter supports multiple programming languages out of the box
Avoiding Perl hacks in Movable Type was a key motivation for seeking a JavaScript solution
Browser compatibility (Firefox and IE6 at the time) was an important consideration for adoption
money_quotes: Although I loves me some Regex, I don't know squat about PERL so I've been hesitant to hack anything fancier into my Movable Type install.
The vanilla PRE sections I've been using are serviceable, but primitive.
It's really quite slick, and it works with all kinds of code.
key_insight: Client-side JavaScript syntax highlighting provides an accessible way for bloggers to improve code presentation without wrestling with server-side templating languages they don't know.
